back_translation: My brothers, do not hold faith in our Lord Jesus Christ who possesses glory, with favoritism toward people
For suppose someone wearing a gold ring and luxurious clothes comes into your meeting, and a poor person wearing tattered clothes also comes in

and you look at the one wearing luxurious clothes with respect, and say, “Please sit here, in this place of honor,” but you say to the poor person, “Stand over there” or “Sit on the floor near my feet”
have you not shown partial-discrimination among yourselves and become judges with evil thoughts?
My beloved brothers, listen — has not God chosen the poor in this world to be rich in faith and heirs of the kingdom He has promised to those who love Him?
But you have despised the poor. Is it not the rich who oppress you, and is it not they themselves who drag you off to court?
Is it not they who blaspheme the noble name that you have received?
However, if you fulfill the law of the great king, as Scripture says, “Love your neighbor as yourself,” you are doing well
But if you show favoritism toward people, you are committing sin, and the law judges you as transgressors
For whoever keeps the entire law but stumbles in one point, that person is guilty of breaking the whole law
For He who said, “Do not commit adultery,” also said, “Do not kill.” If you do not commit adultery but do kill, you have become a transgressor of the law
Speak and act like those who will be judged by the law of freedom
For judgment will be without mercy to one who has not shown mercy. But mercy triumphs over judgment
My brothers, what good is it, if anyone claims to have faith but does not have deeds? Can such faith save him?
Suppose a brother or a sister lacks clothing and lacks food for each day
And one of you says to him, “Go in peace, may you be warm and well fed,” but does not give him the necessary things for his body, what good is that?
Faith too, in the same way, if it does not have deeds, is dead in itself
But someone will say, “You have faith, and I have deeds.” Show me your faith without deeds, and I will show you my faith from my deeds
You believe that God is one? Good. Even the demons believe so, and shudder
O foolish person, do you want to know that faith without deeds is fruitless?
Was not Abraham our forefather declared righteous by his deeds when he offered Isaac his son on the altar?
You see that faith was working together with his deeds, and faith was made complete by those deeds
And the Scripture was fulfilled which says, “Abraham believed God, and He counted it to him as righteousness,” and he was called a friend of God
You all see that a person is declared righteous by deeds, and not by faith alone
In the same way, was not Rahab the prostitute declared righteous by her deeds, when she received the spies and sent them off by another route?
For just as the body without spirit is dead, so faith without deeds is dead
